mission: Reduce the remaining Paper 35 baryogenesis debt after portal-class closure to the exact irreducible objects.

portal_class.portal: V_tr^(I) = y_{i a} epsilon_ab L_i^a H^b N_a + h.c.

formulae.cp_asymmetry: epsilon1 = [1/(8 pi (Y^dagger Y)11)] sum_{j!=1} Im[(Y^dagger Y)1j^2] g(M_j^2/M1^2)
formulae.yield_relation: Y_(B-L) = Integral[ epsilon1 * Gamma_D * source-history functional of f_N ]

theorem_1_cp_and_transfer_common_data_re.statement: Inside the unique type-I portal class, the CP-asymmetry theorem and the visible B-L transfer theorem are not independent remaining debts. Both are determined by the same Yukawa-Majorana data (Y,M), together with the source-abundance history f_N. Therefore the post-classification residual problem reduces exactly to coefficient selection plus source-abundance evolution.

theorem_2_support_branch_nonthermal_nece.statement: At the fixed source scale T_f,target, the standard thermal-radiation Hubble rate exceeds the support-branch local rate by a factor H_rad/H_support. Consequently the Yukawa norm required for thermal equilibrium in the standard radiation bath exceeds the support-equality norm by the same factor. The two regimes are not perturbatively close; the support-branch implementation is necessarily nonthermal relative to the standard leptogenesis bath.

final_boundary.safe_statement: After portal-class closure, the remaining Paper 35 debt reduces exactly to two objects: (i) a theorem selecting Yukawa-Majorana coefficient data inside the type-I portal class, and (ii) a nonthermal support-branch source-abundance theorem. CP asymmetry, visible B-L transfer, and freeze-out are downstream of those two objects.
still_open: a theorem selecting Y and M from IO boundary data
a theorem deriving the nonthermal singlet abundance history on the support branch
